description: Fetch the 1934 human H1 Hemagglutinin complex (PDB: 1RVZ) from the RCSB database into PyMOL. Locate the terminal sialic acid molecules (PDB residue name SIA or NAN). Select all protein residues that have any atom within 4.0 Angstroms of any sialic acid molecule to map the receptor-binding pocket. Create a high-quality visualization focusing on the receptor binding site showing the HA protein pocket, the SIA ligand as sticks, and highlighting the specific contacting protein residues. Render and save the image to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/ha_receptor_binding.png' (resolution at least 800x600). Export a list of all unique protein contact residues (including their Chain, Residue Name, and Residue Number) to a text file at '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/ha_sialic_acid_contacts.txt'. Ensure the report contains the residue numbers of the classic HA receptor-binding motifs (the 130-loop, 190-helix, 220-loop, and the conserved base).
